Insight: DAM prices peak in May (avg ₹5,784/MWh) and reach their lowest in August (avg ₹3,569/MWh). Scheduling generation maintenance during trough months minimises revenue loss.

The top 3 revenue months account for 10.1% of total annual market revenue. High DAM prices during summer months (April–June) align with Khavda's peak solar irradiance, creating a favourable double-upside for market participation.
The highest single-day market revenue of ₹21.71 Lakhs was recorded on 07 Jun 2023, when generation reached 180.9 MW at a DAM clearing price of ₹12,000/MWh.

IEX DAM price volatility stands at 25.7%, indicating significant market price risk. Implementing Power Purchase Agreements (PPAs) for 60–70% of generation capacity is recommended to hedge against spot market price swings.

AI-based forward projections indicate expected market revenue of ₹125.22 Lakhs over the next 13 forecast days, averaging ₹9.63 Lakhs/day. Revenue remains stable given consistent renewable generation under current weather patterns.

With a market price floor of ₹2,385/MWh observed, Khavda's variable generation cost remains well below this threshold, ensuring positive contribution margin under all IEX market clearing scenarios. The average price of ₹4,399/MWh provides a strong margin above typical renewable LCOE of ₹1,800–2,200/MWh.

During the monsoon season (July–September), DAM prices decline by 10–15% due to increased hydroelectric supply. However, Khavda's wind generation surges during this period, partially offsetting the price impact. Operators should consider banking RECs during this window for later monetisation at higher prices.
